_sources.discrepancies: NCSL lists KY as 'non-photo ID requested'. SoS site confirms Kentucky requires a document showing name and address (non-photo acceptable) or a photo ID. Consistent.

earlyVoting.notes: In-person absentee voting (early voting) available the week before the election at the county clerk's office. Hours and locations set by county. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
In-person absentee (early voting) at county clerk's office the week before Election Day. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
In-person absentee (early voting) available at the county clerk's office the week before Election Day. Contact your county clerk for hours and locations.

acceptedIds: Kentucky driver's license or state ID card
Utility bill, bank statement, government check, paycheck, or other government document showing name and address
votingRules.expirationRule: Photo ID should be current. Non-photo documents showing name and address are acceptable even if not expired. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
votingRules.impedimentDeclaration: If a voter cannot provide an acceptable ID, they may cast a provisional ballot and return with ID before 6 p.m. on the second business day after the election to have it counted.
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Kentucky prohibits the use of mobile phones inside polling booths. Phones may generally be used in waiting areas only.
additionalRules: Closed primary: only voters registered with a party may vote in that party's primary. Registered Independents and unaffiliated voters may not vote in a partisan primary.
No legislative primary runoffs in Kentucky.

eligibility: Will be absent from the county on Election Day
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: Must be received by the county clerk 7 days before the election
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by the county clerk by 6 p.m. on Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Absentee ballot must arrive at the county clerk by 6 p.m. on Election Day.

primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEn: Kentucky has a closed primary (Ky. Rev. Stat. §118.245). Only voters registered with a political party may vote in that party's primary. Party affiliation must be set by December 31 of the year before the primary. Voters registered as independent or unaffiliated may not vote in a partisan primary.
primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEs: Kentucky tiene una primaria cerrada (Ky. Rev. Stat. §118.245). Solo los votantes registrados en un partido político pueden votar en la primaria de ese partido. La afiliación de partido debe establecerse antes del 31 de diciembre del año anterior a la primaria. Los votantes registrados como independientes o sin afiliación no pueden votar en una primaria partidista.
